states.name: NormalOperation
states.description: Node is connected to operational network and functioning normally with valid credentials

states.name: ConnectivityLost
states.description: Node has lost connection to operational network due to credential change or network unavailability

states.name: WaitingReconnect
states.description: Node attempting to reconnect to operational network using existing credentials for minimum 120 seconds before entering recovery mode

states.name: RecoveryAnnouncing
states.description: Node in Network Recovery mode, advertising RecoveryIdentifier and attempting reconnection in parallel

state_variables.error_codes: list of error codes leading to recovery need

states.name: ExtendedAnnouncementEnded
states.description: Extended announcement duration has ended but node still cannot reach operational network, continues reconnection at manufacturer-defined intervals

states.name: AwaitingAdministrator
states.description: Node is announcing recovery and Administrator has received announcement but not yet established connection

states.name: RecoveryConnectionEstablished
states.description: Administrator has established connection with Recovery Node over BLE or WiFi commissioning channel

states.name: CASESessionEstablished
states.description: Secure CASE session established between Administrator and Recovery Node, autonomous reconnect attempts suspended

states.name: NodeFailSafeArmed
states.description: Recovery Node has autonomously armed fail-safe timer for 60 seconds upon CASE session establishment

states.name: AdminFailSafeArmed
states.description: Administrator has successfully armed fail-safe timer within 60 seconds of CASE session establishment

states.name: CredentialsProvisioned
states.description: Administrator has provided new operational network credentials to Recovery Node using AddOrUpdateWiFiNetwork or AddOrUpdateThreadNetwork commands

states.name: ConnectingToOperational
states.description: Recovery Node attempting to connect to operational network using newly-provisioned credentials

states.name: OperationalConnected
states.description: Recovery Node successfully connected to operational network with new credentials, recovery connection terminated, all subsequent messages over operational network

states.name: OperationalDiscovered
states.description: Administrator and Recovery Node have discovered each other on operational network using operational discovery mechanism

states.name: RecoveryCompleted
states.description: CommissioningComplete and CommissioningCompleteResponse commands exchanged over CASE session over operational network, recovery successful

states.name: RecoveryAborted
states.description: Network recovery procedure failed or terminated, autonomous reconnection attempts resumed

states.name: FailSafeExpired
states.description: Fail-safe timer expired before recovery completion, state rolled back

transitions.timing_requirement: within 60 seconds of CASE session establishment

functions.name: get_current_time
functions.description: Returns the current system time as a timestamp
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieves current time from system clock in seconds since epoch or similar monotonic time source
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used to record timing of events (connectivity loss, CASE establishment) and calculate time differences for timeout guards

functions.name: start_reconnect_attempts

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Existing operational network credentials to use for reconnection attempts

functions.description: Initiates continuous attempts to reconnect to operational network using provided credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: Starts background process that periodically attempts to authenticate and connect to operational network using the provided WiFi or Thread credentials. Continues until connection succeeds or reconnect_timer expires
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to WaitingReconnect state to begin the mandatory 120-second reconnection attempt period

functions.name: retry_connection

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Credentials to use for connection attempt

functions.description: Performs a single connection attempt to operational network
functions.algorithm_detail: Attempts network association and authentication using provided credentials. For WiFi: attempts WPA2/WPA3 authentication. For Thread: attempts to join Thread network using commissioner credentials. Updates connection status
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called on each tick in WaitingReconnect state to retry connection while timer is active

functions.name: get_recovery_identifier
functions.description: Retrieves the RecoveryIdentifier attribute value from GeneralCommissioning cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Reads the RecoveryIdentifier attribute value from local storage or from the GeneralCommissioning cluster. This identifier uniquely identifies the node for recovery purposes and should have been retrieved by Administrator before credentials changed
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when entering RecoveryAnnouncing state to obtain identifier that will be advertised to Administrator

functions.name: get_error_codes
functions.description: Retrieves error codes that led to the need for network recovery
functions.algorithm_detail: Gathers diagnostic error codes indicating reason for connectivity loss (e.g., authentication failure, network not found, credential mismatch). Returns list of error codes per Matter error code specification
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when entering RecoveryAnnouncing state to populate error_codes field for advertisement

functions.name: get_extended_announcement_duration
functions.description: Returns the Extended Announcement duration value in seconds
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieves the Extended Announcement duration configuration value from specification requirements or implementation settings. This defines how long the node should actively announce recovery availability
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to RecoveryAnnouncing state to initialize announcement_timer

functions.name: start_recovery_announcement

parameters.name: recovery_identifier
parameters.description: RecoveryIdentifier value to advertise

parameters.name: error_codes
parameters.description: Error codes that led to recovery need

functions.description: Begins advertising Network Recovery availability over available commissioning channels
functions.algorithm_detail: Initiates announcement over BLE (using BLE Service Data payload format with RecoveryIdentifier and error codes) and/or WiFi (using WiFi Unsynchronized Service Discovery to publish RecoveryID). Includes OpCode that identifies node as being in Network Recovery mode. Follows announcement duration requirements
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when entering RecoveryAnnouncing state to begin advertising recovery availability to Administrator

functions.name: continue_reconnect_attempts

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Credentials to use for ongoing reconnection attempts

functions.description: Continues autonomous reconnection attempts in parallel with recovery announcement
functions.algorithm_detail: Maintains background reconnection attempts using old credentials while simultaneously advertising recovery availability. Implements parallel execution of two recovery strategies: autonomous reconnection and administrator-assisted recovery
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when entering RecoveryAnnouncing state to ensure node continues attempting autonomous recovery per specification requirement

functions.name: advertise_recovery_payload

parameters.name: recovery_identifier
parameters.description: RecoveryIdentifier to include in advertisement

parameters.name: error_codes
parameters.description: Error codes to include in advertisement

parameters.name: channels
parameters.description: Commissioning channels to use (BLE, WiFi, etc.)

functions.description: Transmits a single recovery advertisement over specified commissioning channels
functions.algorithm_detail: For BLE: broadcasts BLE Service Data payload containing RecoveryIdentifier, OpCode indicating recovery mode, and error codes. For WiFi: publishes using WiFi Unsynchronized Service Discovery with RecoveryID per Table 74. Administrator uses OpCode to distinguish recovery advertisements from commissioning advertisements
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called on each announcement tick in RecoveryAnnouncing state to maintain advertisement visibility

functions.name: attempt_reconnect

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Credentials to use for reconnection attempt

functions.description: Performs a single reconnection attempt to operational network
functions.algorithm_detail: Attempts network association and authentication using provided credentials. Updates internal connection state. If successful, triggers successful_reconnect event. If failed, continues according to current state logic
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called periodically in RecoveryAnnouncing and ExtendedAnnouncementEnded states to maintain parallel reconnection attempts

functions.name: stop_recovery_announcement
functions.description: Stops all active recovery announcements across all commissioning channels
functions.algorithm_detail: Ceases broadcasting BLE Service Data payloads and WiFi discovery publications for recovery. Releases radio resources used for announcement. Called when recovery is no longer needed (successful reconnection or recovery completion)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from RecoveryAnnouncing to NormalOperation upon successful autonomous reconnection

functions.name: stop_active_announcement
functions.description: Stops active announcement when Extended Announcement duration ends
functions.algorithm_detail: Ceases active broadcasting of recovery advertisements but retains recovery state. Node continues periodic reconnection attempts but no longer actively announces availability
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from RecoveryAnnouncing to ExtendedAnnouncementEnded when announcement timer expires

functions.name: continue_periodic_reconnect

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Credentials to use for reconnection

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Manufacturer-defined reconnection interval in seconds

functions.description: Initiates periodic reconnection attempts at manufacturer-defined intervals
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets up timer-based reconnection attempts that occur at the specified interval. Uses exponential backoff or fixed interval as defined by manufacturer. Continues indefinitely until connection succeeds or recovery procedure is restarted
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when entering ExtendedAnnouncementEnded state to maintain reconnection attempts without active announcement

functions.name: restart_recovery_procedure
functions.description: Restarts the entire Network Recovery procedure from announcement phase
functions.algorithm_detail: Resets recovery state and reinitializes announcement timers. Called when CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint steps are performed by user (e.g., button press or reset trigger). Allows user to re-initiate recovery after Extended Announcement has ended
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from ExtendedAnnouncementEnded back to RecoveryAnnouncing upon user-triggered restart

functions.name: establish_recovery_connection
functions.description: Establishes connection with Recovery Node over BLE or WiFi commissioning channel
functions.algorithm_detail: Administrator initiates connection over available commissioning channel (BLE or WiFi Public Action Frame). Establishes transport-level connection similar to commissioning an uncommissioned device. Returns the channel type used (BLE or WiFi)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when Administrator initiates connection after receiving recovery announcement and obtaining user consent

functions.name: get_admin_identity
functions.description: Retrieves Administrator identity information from connection
functions.algorithm_detail: Extracts Administrator identity from connection handshake or session establishment. Returns identifier that can be used for authorization and logging
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing recovery connection to record which Administrator is performing recovery

functions.name: terminate_recovery_flow
functions.description: Terminates the Network Recovery flow when user consent is not granted
functions.algorithm_detail: Closes any open connections, stops announcements, and cleans up recovery state. Per specification, if user consent not granted, Administrator terminates execution and following steps SHALL NOT be performed
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to RecoveryAborted due to lack of user consent

functions.name: resume_autonomous_reconnect

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Credentials to use for autonomous reconnection

functions.description: Resumes autonomous network reconnection attempts after recovery failure
functions.algorithm_detail: Per specification, autonomous attempts to re-establish network connectivity SHALL resume in the event of failure of network recovery procedure. Re-initiates background reconnection process using old credentials
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when recovery fails or is aborted to resume autonomous reconnection attempts as required by specification

functions.name: establish_case_session

parameters.name: admin_identity
parameters.description: Administrator identity for CASE session establishment

functions.description: Establishes secure Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment (CASE) session between Administrator and Recovery Node
functions.algorithm_detail: Implements CASE protocol for mutual authentication using X.509 certificates. Node and Administrator exchange certificates, perform signature verification, and derive shared session keys using Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH). Results in authenticated secure session with derived symmetric encryption keys for subsequent messages
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from RecoveryConnectionEstablished to CASESessionEstablished to secure recovery channel

functions.name: derive_case_keys

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: CASE session from which to derive keys

functions.description: Derives symmetric encryption keys from established CASE session
functions.algorithm_detail: Extracts session key material from CASE handshake. Uses HKDF (HMAC-based Key Derivation Function) to derive separate keys for encryption, authentication, and IV generation. Keys are used with AES-CCM for message encryption and authentication
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after CASE session establishment to obtain keys for encrypting recovery messages per requirement that all messages SHALL be encrypted using CASE-derived keys

functions.name: suspend_autonomous_reconnect
functions.description: Suspends autonomous network reconnection attempts during recovery procedure
functions.algorithm_detail: Per specification, network recovery procedure SHALL preempt autonomous network reconnect attempts. Once secure connection established, ongoing autonomous attempts SHALL be suspended and SHALL NOT interfere with recovery session. Pauses or cancels background reconnection processes
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when CASE session is established to prevent interference between recovery procedure and autonomous reconnection

functions.name: arm_failsafe

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Fail-safe timeout in seconds

functions.description: Arms the fail-safe timer to guard against incomplete recovery
functions.algorithm_detail: Starts fail-safe timer that will trigger automatic rollback if recovery does not complete within timeout period. Analogous to fail-safe in standard commissioning flow. If timer expires without successful completion, node rolls back to previous state
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called automatically by Recovery Node when CASE session established, with 60-second timeout per specification requirement

functions.name: arm_admin_failsafe
functions.description: Administrator arms fail-safe timer on Recovery Node
functions.algorithm_detail: Administrator sends ArmFailSafe command to Recovery Node to confirm continuation of recovery procedure. Must occur within 60 seconds of CASE session establishment. Resets or extends fail-safe timer, indicating Administrator intends to proceed
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when Administrator sends ArmFailSafe command within timing window, transitioning from NodeFailSafeArmed to AdminFailSafeArmed

functions.name: terminate_session
functions.description: Terminates the recovery session and closes connection
functions.algorithm_detail: Closes CASE session, terminates recovery connection over commissioning channel, releases cryptographic session keys securely (zeroing memory), and cleans up connection state. Called when recovery fails or times out
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to RecoveryAborted or FailSafeExpired due to timeout or failure

functions.name: rollback_state
functions.description: Rolls back node state to pre-recovery state when fail-safe expires or recovery fails
functions.algorithm_detail: Reverts any partial configuration changes made during recovery. Removes provisioned credentials if not yet committed, restores old credentials, resets network configuration to state before recovery began. Ensures node is in consistent state after recovery failure
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when fail-safe timer expires or connection to operational network fails, to ensure atomic recovery semantics

functions.name: provision_wifi_credentials

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: WiFi network credentials including SSID, password, security type

functions.description: Provisions new WiFi network credentials received via AddOrUpdateWiFiNetwork command
functions.algorithm_detail: Stores new WiFi credentials (SSID, password, security type, etc.) received from Administrator. Credentials are staged but not yet active until ConnectNetwork command issued. Validates credential format and security type compatibility
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when Administrator sends AddOrUpdateWiFiNetwork command in AdminFailSafeArmed state

functions.name: provision_thread_credentials

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Thread network credentials including operational dataset

functions.description: Provisions new Thread network credentials received via AddOrUpdateThreadNetwork command
functions.algorithm_detail: Stores new Thread operational dataset received from Administrator. Dataset includes network name, PAN ID, extended PAN ID, network key, etc. Credentials staged but not active until ConnectNetwork command. Validates dataset format
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when Administrator sends AddOrUpdateThreadNetwork command in AdminFailSafeArmed state

functions.name: remove_network

parameters.name: network_id
parameters.description: Identifier of network configuration to remove

functions.description: Removes existing network credentials as optional step before provisioning new credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: Administrator MAY choose to delete Recovery Node's existing network credentials by sending RemoveNetwork command. This removes the specified network configuration from node storage. Optional step before provisioning new credentials
functions.usage_in_fsm: Optionally called before credential provisioning to clean up old network configurations

functions.name: provision_credentials

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: WiFi or Thread network credentials

functions.description: Generic function to provision either WiFi or Thread credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: Determines credential type from parameters and calls appropriate provisioning function (provision_wifi_credentials or provision_thread_credentials). Used when credential type is determined at runtime
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition that includes RemoveNetwork followed by credential provisioning

functions.name: attempt_connection_to_operational_network

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: New operational network credentials to use for connection

functions.description: Attempts to connect to operational network using newly-provisioned credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: Per specification, Recovery Node SHALL attempt to connect to operational network using newly-provisioned credentials upon receiving ConnectNetwork command. Performs network association, authentication, and establishes operational connection. Once connected, all remaining messages SHALL be exchanged over operational network
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from CredentialsProvisioned to ConnectingToOperational

functions.name: terminate_recovery_connection
functions.description: Terminates the recovery connection over commissioning channel after operational connection established
functions.algorithm_detail: Closes BLE or WiFi commissioning channel connection that was used for recovery. Per specification, once Recovery Node connects to operational network, recovery connection should be terminated and all subsequent messages exchanged over operational network. Prevents security bypass via dual-channel access
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when successfully connecting to operational network to close recovery channel and force all subsequent communication over operational network

functions.name: perform_operational_discovery
functions.description: Performs mutual operational discovery between Administrator and Recovery Node on operational network
functions.algorithm_detail: Uses operational discovery mechanism per Section 4.3.2 of specification. Administrator and Recovery Node use mDNS or other service discovery to find each other on operational network. Establishes operational addressing and enables CASE session over operational network
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from OperationalConnected to OperationalDiscovered to establish operational communication path

functions.name: send_commissioning_complete_response
functions.description: Sends CommissioningCompleteResponse to Administrator over operational network CASE session
functions.algorithm_detail: Constructs and sends CommissioningCompleteResponse message to Administrator. Message SHALL be sent over CASE session over operational network per specification. Indicates successful completion of Network Recovery flow
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from OperationalDiscovered to RecoveryCompleted upon receiving valid CommissioningComplete command

functions.name: reject_commissioning_complete

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Reason for rejection

functions.description: Rejects CommissioningComplete command that is not received over operational network
functions.algorithm_detail: Per specification, Recovery Node SHALL reject CommissioningComplete that is not received over operational network. Validates that command was received over operational network and over CASE session. If not, rejects command and generates error response
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in stay transition in OperationalDiscovered state when CommissioningComplete received over wrong channel or without proper session

functions.name: reset_recovery_state
functions.description: Resets all recovery-related state variables and cleans up recovery resources
functions.algorithm_detail: Clears recovery_mode flag, resets timers, clears recovery_identifier, removes temporary state, and finalizes transition back to normal operation. Called after successful recovery completion to clean up all recovery resources
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from RecoveryCompleted to NormalOperation to finalize recovery and return to normal operational state

definitions.definition: An attribute of the GeneralCommissioning cluster that uniquely identifies a node for Network Recovery purposes. Administrator retrieves this value before operational network credentials change to enable identification of the correct node during recovery.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for preventing credential provisioning to wrong devices. Non-unique or predictable RecoveryIdentifiers enable imposter attacks where attacker receives credentials meant for victim node.

definitions.definition: A protocol for establishing mutually authenticated secure sessions using X.509 certificates. Both parties verify each other's certificates and derive shared session keys using ECDH for subsequent encrypted communication.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides authentication and confidentiality for recovery messages. All recovery messages MUST be encrypted with CASE-derived keys to prevent credential interception. CASE session over commissioning channel must be used only for recovery, not steady-state operations.

definitions.definition: A countdown timer that triggers automatic rollback if recovery procedure does not complete within timeout period. Recovery Node autonomously arms for 60 seconds upon CASE session establishment; Administrator must re-arm within 60 seconds to continue.
definitions.security_relevance: Guards against incomplete recovery leaving node in inconsistent state. Prevents Administrator abandonment from causing permanent misconfiguration. Ensures atomic recovery semantics.

definitions.definition: A period during which Recovery Node actively announces its recovery availability over commissioning channels. Duration is defined by specification or implementation. After expiry, node continues reconnection attempts but stops active announcement.
definitions.security_relevance: Limits exposure window of RecoveryIdentifier to reduce attack opportunities. Balances recoverability with minimizing wireless spectrum pollution and security exposure.

definitions.definition: Service discovery mechanism (Section 4.3.2) for nodes to find each other on operational network after connection established. Uses mDNS or similar to enable Administrator and Recovery Node to establish operational communication path.
definitions.security_relevance: Required to establish operational CASE session for CommissioningComplete exchange. Ensures final recovery steps occur over operational network, preventing premature completion attacks via recovery channel.

definitions.definition: A set of steps (e.g., button press, device reset) that trigger restart of Network Recovery procedure after Extended Announcement ends. Allows user to manually re-initiate recovery.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides recovery from Extended Announcement timeout without requiring device reset. User-controlled trigger ensures legitimate recovery attempts can be restarted.

definitions.definition: Background process where Recovery Node continuously attempts to connect to operational network using old credentials. Runs in parallel with recovery announcement and is suspended during active recovery procedure.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables recovery from transient network outages without Administrator intervention. Must be suspended during recovery to prevent interference with recovery procedure. Resumes upon recovery failure per specification.

definitions.definition: Explicit user approval obtained by Administrator before provisioning credentials to Recovery Node. Required to prevent credential provisioning to imposter devices or during temporary outages when user doesn't intend recovery.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical security control to prevent unauthorized credential provisioning. Administrator SHALL obtain consent and SHALL NOT proceed without it. Prevents automated systems from provisioning attackers.

definitions.definition: The primary WiFi or Thread network on which Matter nodes communicate during normal operation. Distinguished from commissioning channels (BLE, WiFi Public Action Frame) used during setup and recovery.
definitions.security_relevance: Final recovery steps MUST occur over operational network to prevent Man-in-the-Middle attacks. CommissioningComplete MUST be over operational network and CASE session, rejected otherwise.

definitions.definition: Connection established over commissioning channel (BLE or WiFi) between Administrator and Recovery Node for recovery procedure. Terminated once operational network connection established.
definitions.security_relevance: Must be terminated after operational connection to prevent security bypass via dual-channel access. Attacker with physical proximity could send commands over recovery channel to bypass operational network security.

security_properties.description: All messages exchanged over recovery connection are encrypted using CASE-derived keys, preventing eavesdropping on network credentials
CASE session establishes mutual authentication between Administrator and Recovery Node using certificates before credential exchange
Administrator must obtain user consent before provisioning credentials, preventing automated attacks and wrong device provisioning
CommissioningComplete must be received over operational network and CASE session, rejected otherwise, preventing premature completion attacks
Network recovery procedure preempts autonomous reconnect attempts once CASE session established, preventing interference and race conditions
Fail-safe timer ensures atomic recovery by triggering rollback if recovery does not complete within timeout, preventing inconsistent state
Recovery Node must attempt reconnection for at least 120 seconds before entering recovery mode, avoiding unnecessary announcements for transient outages
Recovery connection over commissioning channel is terminated once operational connection established, preventing dual-channel security bypass
Autonomous reconnection attempts resume if recovery fails, ensuring node continues attempting recovery per specification requirement
Node attempts autonomous reconnection in parallel with recovery announcement, maximizing recovery success probability

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment (CASE) protocol using X.509 certificates and ECDH key exchange
cryptographic_operations.output: Authenticated secure session with derived symmetric session keys (encryption, authentication, IV generation keys)
assumptions: X.509 certificates are trusted and validly signed by trusted Certificate Authority
Certificate private keys are securely stored and not leaked
Random number generator for ephemeral keys and nonces is cryptographically secure

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: AES-CCM (Counter with CBC-MAC) for authenticated encryption
inputs: Initialization Vector (IV) derived from CASE session
cryptographic_operations.output: Ciphertext with authentication tag, providing confidentiality and integrity
assumptions: AES-CCM provides authenticated encryption with associated data (AEAD)
Session keys are not leaked or reused across sessions
IVs are never reused with same key
Authentication tag verification is always performed before using decrypted data

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: HKDF (HMAC-based Key Derivation Function) with SHA-256
inputs: Shared secret from ECDH key exchange
cryptographic_operations.output: Separate symmetric keys for encryption, authentication, and IV generation
assumptions: HKDF provides cryptographic separation of derived keys
Different Info strings ensure key independence
Shared secret from ECDH has sufficient entropy

security_assumptions.assumption: CASE protocol is secure and provides mutual authentication with forward secrecy
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker can impersonate Administrator or Recovery Node, decrypt recovery messages, and extract network credentials. Man-in-the-middle attacks become feasible.

security_assumptions.assumption: RecoveryIdentifier is globally unique across all nodes in all fabrics
security_assumptions.if_violated: Non-unique RecoveryIdentifiers allow attacker to impersonate victim node by advertising colliding identifier and receiving credentials meant for victim.

security_assumptions.assumption: User can correctly identify legitimate nodes requiring recovery and distinguish from imposter devices
security_assumptions.if_violated: User may grant consent for credential provisioning to attacker-controlled devices advertising fake RecoveryIdentifiers.

security_assumptions.assumption: Administrator is not compromised and behaves according to specification
security_assumptions.if_violated: Compromised Administrator can provision malicious credentials, extract RecoveryIdentifiers for later attacks, or deny service by refusing legitimate recovery.

security_assumptions.assumption: Fail-safe mechanism reliably triggers rollback when timer expires
security_assumptions.if_violated: Incomplete recovery procedures leave node in inconsistent state with partial credential updates, requiring manual intervention to restore functionality.

security_assumptions.assumption: Operational discovery mechanism is secure and cannot be spoofed
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker can inject fake discovery responses causing Administrator and Recovery Node to establish operational CASE session with attacker-controlled endpoint.

security_assumptions.assumption: No race conditions exist between recovery channel messages and operational network messages
security_assumptions.if_violated: Node may accept CommissioningComplete over recovery channel before operational network transition completes, or process commands from both channels simultaneously.

security_assumptions.assumption: Recovery connection is reliably terminated after operational connection establishment
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker with physical proximity can send commands over recovery channel (BLE/WiFi) after node is on operational network, bypassing network security policies.

security_assumptions.assumption: Clock drift or lack of time synchronization does not significantly affect timeout durations (120s, 60s)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Nodes may enter recovery mode too early/late, fail-safe may expire prematurely causing legitimate recovery to fail, or automatic fail-safe may expire before Administrator can re-arm.

security_assumptions.assumption: Network connectivity loss is reliably detectable and distinguishable from transient failures
security_assumptions.if_violated: False positives cause unnecessary recovery mode entry exposing RecoveryIdentifier. False negatives prevent recovery when actually needed leaving node permanently disconnected.

security_assumptions.assumption: BLE and WiFi commissioning channels are available and functional on both Administrator and Recovery Node
security_assumptions.if_violated: Recovery mechanism cannot function. Nodes become unrecoverable without physical access or out-of-band recovery methods.

security_assumptions.assumption: Autonomous reconnection attempts do not interfere with recovery procedure when suspended
security_assumptions.if_violated: Race conditions between autonomous reconnection and recovery procedure cause unpredictable behavior, partial configuration updates, or connection to wrong network.
